sn21.8:1.0.1: At Sāvatthī. Then the Venerable Nanda, the Blessed One's maternal cousin, put on well-pressed and well-ironed robes, painted his eyes, took a glazed bowl, and approached the Blessed One.Having paid homage to the Blessed One, he sat down to one side, and the Blessed One said to him:
sn21.8:1.0.3: Then, some time later, the Venerable Nanda became a forest dweller, an almsfood eater, a rag-robes wearer, and he dwelt indifferent to sensual pleasures.
